rulings.Case Title: RENE DEL PRADO VS. BENNETT HONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Hearing On Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 28, 2022 line 1. PETITIONER RENE DEL PRADO'S AMENDED NOTICE OF HEARING ON WRIT OF MANDATE is OFF CALENDAR, no proof of service. Furthermore, an amended petition for Writ of Mandate on file. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 28, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANTS DAVID BELLINGS AND NRT WEST, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist regarding the disclosures, justifiable reliance and causation. Such facts include but are not limited to the changes in the 2017 disclosures and additional facts 23-25. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or Summary Adjudication To Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 28, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANTS KIM MAXWELL AND JUDITH MAXWELL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist regarding the disclosures. Such facts include but are not limited to the changes in the 2017 disclosures and additional fact 24. Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied for an additional reason that the moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b) and repeat the issues listed in the Notice verbatim in the Separate Statement. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CLEAR HOMES LLC, VS. MIGUEL GARCIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 28, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANTS MIGUEL GARCIA AND DANELIA ESTRADA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. Plaintiff complied with SFRO 37.9A(f)(4)(E)(iii); any issues regarding the name and identity of the managing member are not properly raised at the pleading stage. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: JOANNA LUO VS. SNOWY DU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 28, 2022 line 11. DEFENDANTS SNOWY DU, DEREK KONG, RUIBO ZHENG AND GUOCHAO DU'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to comply with SFRO 37.9(a)(8)(v) and failed to include all the required information in the declaration accompanying the notice. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that the undeclared part of the document meets the requirement to "include a declaration executed by the landlord under the penalty of perjury stating the landlord's relative (identified by name and relation to the landlord)." =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOSE INIGUEZ ET AL VS. AGUSTIN CRUZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 28, 2022 line 12. DEFENDANTS AGUSTIN CRUZ, REYNA GONZALEZ, CRYSTIAN MENDEZ GONZALEZ, MARIA AGUILAR AND DARWIN GOMEZ'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED, 5 days to answer. Attached to the original complaint was (as correctly pointed out by the defendant) a document that purported to be a lease, but was not legible and appeared to be incomplete. That attachment does not contradict Plaintiff's new allegation that Plaintiff is not in possession of the Lease (legible, binding, complete, etc). =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOSE INIGUEZ ET AL VS. AGUSTIN CRUZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 28, 2022 line 12. DEFENDANTS AGUSTIN CRUZ, REYNA GONZALEZ, CRYSTIAN MENDEZ GONZALEZ, MARIA AGUILAR AND DARWIN GOMEZ'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. Attached to the original complaint was (as correctly pointed out by the defendant) a document that purported to be a lease, but was not legible and appeared to be incomplete. That attachment does not contradict Plaintiff's new allegation that Plaintiff is not in possession of the Lease (legible, binding, complete, etc). =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
